summary.intent: Fix CI failures on PR #724 in the ars-ui repo, specifically the Leptos Button adapter test regressions caused by a reactive class contract change, then re-trigger Codex review and push to merge.
summary.outcome: The stale static-class assertions in the Leptos Button unit test were updated to materialize reactive AttrValue instead of reading raw static strings. All four CI feature variants (all-features, SSR, hydrate, CSR) passed locally, and the fix was committed and pushed to PR #724.

repo: merge_consumer_class_prop_into emits a reactive class value, not a static string attr — AttrMap::get will not find it as a static entry
CI runs the Leptos adapter tests across four feature shapes: all-features, ssr, hydrate, and csr — fixes must be validated against all four
Codex review is triggered via gh pr comment with '@codex review' and monitored via reactions and GraphQL reviewThreads API
The repo uses a commit hook that must complete cleanly before push; hook output should be waited on rather than interrupted
cargo xclippy is the project's clippy gate; a known upstream future-incompat warning from block v0.1.6 is expected and non-blocking

code.finding: Two assertions in user_root_attrs_are_applied_and_as_child_native_attrs_are_filtered used AttrMap::get to check the class attr as a static string, but after the class-prop cleanup the class is emitted as a reactive AttrValue that must be materialized rather than read as a raw static entry
AttrValue has a materialize_string() or equivalent method used to resolve reactive attr values for test assertions

workflow: Use gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/jobs/{job_id}/logs to pull raw CI job logs for precise failure diagnosis
Use gh api graphql with reviewThreads to poll Codex feedback without missing new review comments
Poll CI and Codex in a loop with sleep intervals (90-120s) rather than blocking on --watch when multiple slow jobs are pending
Reproduce the exact failing test name and feature flag combination locally before editing production or test code
Run cargo fmt --all -- --check and cargo xclippy as a gate before committing CI fixes

friction: Codex review requires a fresh '@codex review' trigger comment after every new push — it does not automatically re-review on force-push or new commit
CI matrix runs the same test across four feature shapes (all-features, ssr, hydrate, csr), requiring explicit local reproduction of each shape
The commit hook runs silently for an extended period with no progress output, requiring passive waiting without feedback
gh pr checks --watch=false does not block, so CI state must be polled manually in a loop with sleep intervals
The GraphQL reviewThreads query must be constructed and sent manually each poll cycle — no built-in gh CLI shortcut
open_items: Codex review was still pending (eyes reaction, no new threads) at the time of the final push — approval outcome is unresolved
The broader adapter/coverage/feature CI jobs were still pending when the session ended — full green CI not yet confirmed
The upstream block v0.1.6 future-incompat warning from cargo xclippy is known but unaddressed
